Watchdog driver implementation for nRF52840, including initialization, kicking, and reset flag check.

# Purpose
The code manages the functionality of a watchdog timer on an nRF52840 microcontroller. It includes functions to initialize the watchdog, periodically reset it to prevent it from timing out, and check if a reset was caused by the watchdog. The [`watchdog_init`](<#watchdog_init>) function configures the watchdog timer with a timeout interval of 8 seconds and starts it. Once started, the watchdog cannot be stopped and will continue to run even after a system reset. The [`watchdog_kick`](<#watchdog_kick>) function resets the watchdog timer to prevent it from expiring, ensuring that the system does not reset unexpectedly. It checks all possible reload registers to ensure that any enabled register is reset. The [`watchdog_check_clear_reset_flag`](<#watchdog_check_clear_reset_flag>) function checks if the last reset was caused by the watchdog and clears the reset reason flags.

This code is intended to be part of a larger system where maintaining system stability and preventing unwanted resets during critical operations is necessary. It interacts with the hardware-specific registers of the nRF52840 to control the watchdog timer. The code is not a standalone executable but is likely part of a firmware library that provides watchdog management functionality to other parts of the system. It does not define public APIs or external interfaces but provides essential internal functions for watchdog management.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/watchdog.h`
- `helpers/nrfx_reset_reason.h`
- `nrfx.h`


# Functions

---
### watchdog\_init<!-- {{#callable:watchdog_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../platform/asterix/boot/src/drivers/watchdog.c#L19>)

Initializes the watchdog timer (WDT) to run during sleep and halt during debugging, with an 8-second expiration period.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the WDT configuration to run during sleep and pause during debugging by writing to `NRF_WDT->CONFIG`.
    - Enables the reload register 0 by writing to `NRF_WDT->RREN`.
    - Sets the WDT expiration period to 8 seconds by writing to `NRF_WDT->CRV`.
    - Starts the WDT by writing to `NRF_WDT->TASKS_START`.
    - Notes that the WDT cannot be stopped after starting, even surviving a system reset.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.


---
### watchdog\_kick<!-- {{#callable:watchdog_kick}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../platform/asterix/boot/src/drivers/watchdog.c#L34>)

Resets all enabled watchdog reload registers to prevent the watchdog timer from expiring.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the watchdog timer is running by evaluating `NRF_WDT->RUNSTATUS`.
    - If the watchdog is running, iterates over all 8 possible reload registers.
    - For each register, checks if it is enabled by evaluating `NRF_WDT->RREN & (1 << i)`.
    - If a register is enabled, sets `NRF_WDT->RR[i]` to `WDT_RR_RR_Reload` to reset it.
- **Output**: No output; the function operates on hardware registers to reset the watchdog timer.


---
### watchdog\_check\_clear\_reset\_flag<!-- {{#callable:watchdog_check_clear_reset_flag}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../platform/asterix/boot/src/drivers/watchdog.c#L46>)

Checks if the watchdog reset flag is set and clears all reset reasons.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call `nrfx_reset_reason_get()` to retrieve the reset reason.
    - Call `nrfx_reset_reason_clear(0xFFFFFFFF)` to clear all reset reasons.
    - Return `true` if the reset reason includes the watchdog reset flag (`NRFX_RESET_REASON_DOG_MASK`), otherwise return `false`.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether the watchdog reset flag was set.
